title: The Evolution of Java GUI Toolkits Leading to Swing
description: A timeline showing the progression of Java GUI frameworks from AWT to Swing, highlighting the motivation for Swing's creation.
relationships: AWT's heavy reliance on native peers led to performance and consistency issues
Swing was developed as a successor to address AWT's shortcomings with a pure-Java, lightweight component set
Swing adopted a Model-View-Controller (MVC) architecture for greater flexibility and a pluggable look-and-feel
